summary: Do not ship: RL-2 and RL-3 are fixed, but RL-1 remains open in two release-blocking ways. No new critical issue was found.
triage: review-gate SKILL.md rules govern: render EVERY finding as a full table row, the human decides each row (accept/reject/defer/question — free text), echo back before applying, max 2 rounds, append decisions.md and COMMIT it with this artifact.

findings.title: RL-1 still exposes trust-anchor tokens through shell history
findings.body: The helper claims tokens never enter argv, URLs, or history, but it accepts each token as `$1` and instructs the operator to replace quoted placeholders in the command. Those literal PAT and App tokens are recorded by normal interactive shell history before GIT_ASKPASS is involved. GIT_ASKPASS only keeps them out of Git's URL and process arguments.
findings.recommendation: Load both credentials from the approved secret environment and make the helper consume them without secret-valued function arguments. The entered commands should contain only environment-variable names; unset the variables afterward.

findings.title: RL-1 can falsely certify update enforcement after writer creation fails
findings.body: B3 does not abort or otherwise prove that the writer-created ref exists. If that push fails, B4 targets a nonexistent ref and therefore exercises creation again, not update. The generic rejection matcher then accepts the creation ruleset error and reports it as successful update enforcement, allowing authoritative Seam C evidence to certify an untested control.
findings.recommendation: Make B3 failure abort the procedure, verify the exact remote ref and expected OID with `git ls-remote` before B4, and require B4 to return nonzero plus a ruleset-specific rejection before recording success.

next_steps: Repair the two RL-1 procedure gaps and re-run the scoped release re-review.
Retain the RL-2 and RL-3 changes; both are correctly addressed within the accepted scope.
